questions.text: Why does the Surah focus on the earth shaking and speaking?
questions.answer: To show that even what seems inanimate will obey Allah and expose human deeds.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that minor deeds are ignored by Allah.
questions.answer: False. Even an atom’s weight of good or evil will be seen.

questions.text: What does the earth bringing out its ‘burdens’ teach about resurrection?
questions.answer: The dead and hidden matters will be brought out; nothing stays buried from Allah’s decree.

questions.text: What does the human question 'What is wrong with it?' teach about the shock of that Day?
questions.answer: People will be overwhelmed because the familiar world becomes unrecognizable and exposes reality.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach that the earth’s testimony is by Allah’s command?
questions.answer: It shows Allah inspires and commands creation; testimony is part of His perfect justice.

questions.text: What is one practical meaning of people coming in scattered groups?
questions.answer: Each person is separated for accountability; social masks and group identity do not save.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that your environment has no role in your accountability.
questions.answer: False. It teaches the earth itself witnesses actions and will testify.

questions.text: What is one daily-life lesson from the earth being a witness?
questions.answer: Avoid sins in private places; nothing is truly hidden from Allah’s record.

questions.text: How can this Surah motivate consistent small good deeds?
questions.answer: It teaches even tiny good deeds are seen, so small consistent الخير matters greatly.

questions.text: What is one example of an ‘atom’s weight’ of good?
questions.answer: A kind word, removing harm from the road, or a sincere dua for someone.

questions.text: What is one example of an ‘atom’s weight’ of evil?
questions.answer: A small lie, a hurtful comment, or a minor ظلم that people dismiss as ‘nothing’.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that only public crimes are accountable.
questions.answer: False. It implies all deeds, even tiny and hidden ones, are shown.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach precision of divine justice?
questions.answer: By stating the smallest measurable deeds will be shown without loss.

questions.text: How can this Surah help someone who feels their good deeds are insignificant?
questions.answer: It reassures that Allah sees every good deed, no matter how small.

questions.text: How can this Surah help someone who takes small sins lightly?
questions.answer: It warns that every deed will be shown, so small sins still require repentance.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests the Day of Judgment is only about major events, not personal deeds.
questions.answer: False. It focuses on personal deeds down to the smallest weight.

questions.text: What is one daily practice inspired by this Surah?
questions.answer: Do a nightly micro-audit: repent for one small wrong and do one small good deed intentionally.

questions.text: How does this Surah connect fear and hope?
questions.answer: Fear: every sin is seen. Hope: every good deed is seen and rewarded.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah’s power over creation?
questions.answer: Allah can command earth itself to testify, proving His absolute authority.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that people can escape by blending into crowds.
questions.answer: False. People are shown their deeds and judged individually.

questions.text: What does 'Az-Zalzalah' mean?
questions.answer: The Earthquake / The Shaking.

questions.text: How is the earthquake of the Final Day described in this Surah?
questions.answer: As a 'final/utmost' shaking (Zilzalaha).

questions.text: What will the earth bring out from within itself?
questions.answer: Its 'burdens' (Athqalaha) - meaning the dead or its hidden secrets.

questions.text: What will the human being ask in wonder according to this Surah?
questions.answer: What is [wrong] with it (the earth)?

questions.text: What will the earth do on that Day according to this Surah?
questions.answer: It will report its news / tell its stories.

questions.text: Why will the earth be able to speak or report news?
questions.answer: Because your Lord has inspired (commanded) it to do so.

questions.text: How will mankind proceed on that Day according to this Surah?
questions.answer: In 'scattered groups' (Ashtatan).

questions.text: What is the purpose of people being in groups/scattered?
questions.answer: To be shown [the result of] their deeds.

questions.text: What happens to the one who does an atom's weight of good?
questions.answer: He will see it (and be rewarded).

questions.text: What happens to the one who does an atom's weight of evil?
questions.answer: He will see it (and be held accountable).

questions.text: What is a 'Mithqala Dharratin'?
questions.answer: The weight of an atom, a tiny ant, or the smallest particle imaginable.

questions.text: Does any deed go missing in Allah's account?
questions.answer: No, even the smallest act is recorded.

questions.text: What is the significance of the earth 'reporting news'?
questions.answer: The earth witnesses every action committed upon it and will testify for or against people.

questions.text: How does this Surah evoke a sense of accountability?
questions.answer: By showing that the very ground we walk on is a silent observer that will eventually speak.

questions.text: What is the 'burden' (Athqal) referring to?
questions.answer: The bodies of the dead being resurrected and the treasures/secrets of the earth.

questions.text: True or False: On the Day of Judgment, people will be separated according to their deeds.
questions.answer: True (Ashtatan).

questions.text: What is the emotional impact of the question 'What is wrong with it?'
questions.answer: It captures the shock and total confusion of humans when the world they knew begins to disintegrate.

questions.text: Is there any 'neutral' deed that isn't rewarded or punished?
questions.answer: The Surah implies all deeds fall into either good or evil and will be shown.

questions.text: Why did Allah inspire the earth to speak?
questions.answer: To show His supreme power over all of creation, even inanimate matter.

questions.text: What is the primary message of Surah Az-Zalzalah?
questions.answer: The absolute precision of divine justice and the reality of resurrection.
